CP00-259 (descriptor-only RP07 search-core permission-binding slice, risk B, 40 units) is internally consistent and honestly scoped. The pack does **not** overclaim: `production_ready:false` / `implemented_pending_review` in both manifest and command-evidence, with Claude held as read-only reviewer and human final approval required.

Verified at source level:
- Scope, deliverable (3/18/7/7/4/1) and micro-title (2/25/13) distributions match the baseline across registry binding, requirements, contract, plan pack, and manifest.
- All 7 public exports exist and re-export via index.js; the CP259 code is pure frozen-object construction with every runtime/write/audit/storage flag forced false — no I/O introduced, so the no-write/no-real-data boundary holds.
- Validator + 3 tests assert coverage.valid, slice.valid, deny-over-allow, no bypass, human approval required, `claude_is_final_approval:false`, read-only, and CP00-260 handoff.
- CP178+ hardening (auth-failure/zero-byte/malformed-JSON/fenced-JSON/tool-call-shaped/missing-field rejections) is encoded; Hermes is used only as deterministic packet/receipt descriptors, not product runtime.

Findings: **0 P0 / 0 P1 / 0 P2 / 1 P3** — a non-blocking, by-design note that the post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are generated after this review by the hardened sequence; the human approver should confirm they and the final gated commands complete before commit. Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack-level claim is honest and supported: manifest and command-evidence report production_ready=false / implemented_pending_review (no enterprise-trust overclaim). Pack binding, requirements, contract current_pack, plan pack, and manifest all agree on scope; deliverable distribution (3/18/7/7/4/1) and micro-title distribution (2/25/13) match the baseline exactly. All 7 required public exports exist across service.js/validators.js and surface via index.js wildcard re-exports. The CP259 code is pure frozen-object construction: freezeCp259Result forces every runtime/dispatch/write/audit/storage flag false, rows are descriptor_only with runtime_execution=false, and no I/O, fetch, fs, exec, or DB was introduced — so the no-write/no-real-data boundary holds. The descriptor validator's assertions all resolve against ROW_EXTRAS inherited through CP256-CP258 and against the no-write attestation (permission_bypass_detected, promotes_claude_to_final_approval, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation all false). The validator script and 3 model tests assert coverage.valid, slice.valid, deny-over-allow enforced, no bypass, human_final_approval_required, claude_is_final_approval=false, read_only=true, and handoff to CP00-260. Authority boundary is preserved throughout (Claude read-only, not final approver; human final approval required). CP178+ review hardening is encoded via forbidden_review_evidence (auth-failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped, missing-field rejections) and a hardened_review_sequence that runs Claude before adjudication/inspection/closeout; readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON. Hermes patterns are used only as deterministic evidence-packet/receipt descriptors (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. No P0/P1/P2 findings; one P3 informational note about post-review artifacts that are produced after this review by design.
